steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Translate "$x$ is 37% of $y$" into an equation, then solve for $y$ in terms of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Writing the percentage statement as an equation gives $x=\frac{37}{100}y$. Multiply both sides by $\frac{100}{37}$ to isolate $y$: $y=\frac{100}{37}x$.
  • Choice A — $\frac{100}{37}x$: matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $\frac{10}{37}x$: off by a factor of 10; it would correspond to a different percent relationship, not $x$ being $37\%$ of $y$. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{1}{37}x$: omits the factor of 100 entirely; this would give $y$ in terms of a 3700% relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{63}{100}x$: uses $100-37=63$, confusing "37% of $y$" with "$y$ minus 37% of $y$". ✗
A is correct.
